content.full_text:
August 3, 1976

Mr. James M. Paxton
Coughlin, Wyckoff, Parker
and Paxton
P.O. Box 8
Hollister, California 95023

Dear Mr. Paxton:

This is in response to your letter dated July 26
regarding the Conflict of Interest Code for the Pacheco
Pass Water District and the requirements of subsection (d)
(4) of 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18750.

The aforementioned regulation requires that a Conflict
of Interest Code submitted to the Fair Political Practices
Commission be accompanied by:

". . . materials describing generally the reasons
for the designations and the disclosure responsibi-
lities of officers, employees, members or consultants
of the agency. . ."

The Commission, as a code reviewing body, is charged
with the responsibility of assuring that a Code not only
discloses or prevents all foreseeable potential Conflict of
Interest situations [§87309(a)], but also differentiates
between designated employees with different powers and
responsibilities [§87309(c)]. The justification materials
required by the Regulation §18700(d)(4) are essential if the
code reviewing body is to adequately review the Codes before
it. The Commission must have some knowledge as to why a
particular position was "designated", and why designated
employees must disclose particular types of interests. These
materials, moreover, are especially important in view of the
Commission's current concern that an expansive reading of
2 Cal. Adm. Code §18700(c), a regulation defining "participates
in the making of a governmental decision", has resulted in the
designation of employees who do not, in any real sense, parti-
cipate in decision-making which will foreseeably affect their
financial interests.

[illegible] ANURA

[illegible]

[illegible] N [illegible]
[illegible] [illegible]
[illegible]
[illegible] [illegible]

[illegible] [illegible]

[illegible] [illegible] [illegible]

Page 2
8-3-76
SS:jhm


We regret any confusion caused by the Commission's
release, dated April 26, 1976, requesting code drafters to
submit materials justifying code designation in addition to
the materials required by §18750(d)(4). Although the
submission accompanying the Pacheco Pass Code is adequate
in all other respects, the statement regarding the desig-
nation of all officials, employees, members, and consultants
of the agency provides insufficient information to our agency,
and further supportive materials will be required.

    I am enclosing copies of materials received from other
water districts as examples of an appropriate justification
statement. Also enclosed are notices concerning Conflict of
Interest Code development, in light of which you may wish to
delay submission of the justification materials.

    If you have further questions, do not hesitate to
contact me at (916)322-6444.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Sharon Stevens
                                        Conflicts of Interest
                                        Division

SS:jhm

Enc. (4)

[The document appears to be a typewritten page on green/light colored paper with two holes punched at the top. The text is difficult to read due to the image quality and appears to be upside down or reversed. Due to the poor legibility and reversed/inverted nature of the text in this image, I cannot provide an accurate verbatim transcription. The document appears to contain several paragraphs of text, but the specific words and content are not clearly distinguishable enough to transcribe with confidence while maintaining accuracy.]

COUGHLIN, WYCKOFF, PARKER & PAXTON
A LAW CORPORATION
390 FIFTH STREET · P.O. BOX 8
HOLLISTER, CALIFORNIA 95023
TELEPHONE 408-637-3706

AT ARTOA
WYCKOFF, PARKER,
BOYLE & POPE
7873 SOQUEL DRIVE

AT WATSONVILLE
WYCKOFF, PARKER,
BOYLE & POPE
14 CARR STREET

July 26, 1976

Ms. Sharon Stevens
Conflicts of Interest Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
State of California
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Pacheco Pass Water District
A California Water District

Dear Ms. Stevens:

Your letter to me dated June 28, 1976, indicates that
our submission to the Commission on May 27, 1976 did not
comply with the Administrative Code Section 18750 because
materials describing the reasons for designations and dis-
closure responsibilities were not included.

It is our understanding from the Commission's release
dated April 26, 1976, that the Commission is concerned with
materials supporting a determination not to designate
officials who could arguably be within the coverage of a
Conflict of Interest Code. In the paragraph numbered 5
of the Declaration accompanying our proposed code and
included in our letter to you dated May 27, 1976 is a
statement indicating that the designation of officers,
employees, members, and consultants includes all officers,
employees, members and consultants of the agency. No one
falling within any of these catagories is excluded from the
designation of those by whom disclosure statements should
be filed. None of the parties so designated objected at
the public hearing held for that purpose to the agency's
determination that all should file disclosure statements.
As we have included everyone, I would not think that there
is any public concern with the reasons for doing so, as
there might be if certain persons falling within the
categories mentioned were excluded from the disclosure
requirement. The designation of all officers, employees,
members, and consultants of the district was made under
our interpretation of 2 California Administrative Code
Section 18700(c) and, because of the small size of our
district, all of such parties may be involved in the making

[The image appears to be almost entirely blank or extremely faint. I can only make out what appears to be two small circular or crescent-shaped marks near the top of the page, but no legible text is visible enough to transcribe accurately.]

Ms. Sharon Stevens
Conflicts of Interest Division
July 26, 1976
Page 2


Of governmental decisions which may foreseeably have a
material effect on some financial interest of theirs.

If you wish any further materials to support our
submission, please advise me, more particularly what
materials you require. If you now consider our submission
to be satisfactory, please advise me of that, also.

Very truly yours,

JAMES M. PAXTON

JMP:ab

cc: Mrs. Susan Hodges
Secretary
Pacheco Pass Water District

[The image appears to be largely blank/white with only some very faint markings or artifacts visible at the top and bottom. The content is not legible enough to transcribe with confidence.]

Box 8 Hollister, California 95023 Dear Mr. Paxton: This is in response to your letter dated July 26 regarding the Conflict of Interest Code for the Pacheco Pass Water District and the requirements of subsection (d) (4) of 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18750. The aforementioned regulation requires that a Conflict of Interest Code submitted to the Fair Political Practices Commission be accompanied by: "... materials describing generally the reasons for the designations and the disclosure responsibilities of officers, employees, members or consultants of the agency. . ." The Commission, as a code reviewing body, is charged with the responsibility of assuring that a Code not only discloses or prevents all foreseeable potential Conflict of Interest situations [§87309(a)], but also differentiates between designated employees with different powers and responsibilities [§87309(c)]. The justification materials required by the Regulation §18700(d)(4) are essential if the code reviewing body is to adequately review the Codes before it. The Commission must have some knowledge as to why a particular position was "designated", and why designated employees must disclose particular types of interests. These materials, moreover, are especially important in view of the Commission's current concern that an expansive reading of 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18700(c), a regulation defining "participates in the making of a governmental decision", has resulted in the designation of employees who do not, in any real sense, participate in decision-making which will foreseeably affect their financial interests. The image appears to be a photograph of a handwritten letter, but the text is extremely faint and mostly illegible. Only a few scattered words and numbers are partially visible, such as "Dear Mr. Textron," "Hollister, California 95023," and some numbers and letters that are not fully legible. The majority of the content cannot be reliably transcribed due to the quality and clarity issues. If you have another scan or a higher quality image, please provide it for better results. We regret any confusion caused by the Commission's release, dated April 26, 1976, requesting code drafters to submit materials justifying non-designation in addition to the materials required by §18750(d)(4). Although the submission accompanying the Pacheco Pass Code is adequate in all other respects, the statement regarding the designation of all officials, employees, members, and consultants of the agency provides insufficient information to our agency, and further supportive materials will be required. I am enclosing copies of materials received from other water districts as examples of an appropriate justification statement. Also enclosed are notices concerning Conflict of Interest Code development, in light of which you may wish to delay submission of the justification materials. If you have further questions, do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-6444. Sincerely, Sharon Stevens Conflicts of Interest Division SS:jhm Enc.
